title: Mobile malware evolution in 2025
summary: Statistics on Android malware and the most notable mobile threats of 2025: preinstalled backdoors Keenadu and Triada, spyware Trojans, the Kimwolf IoT botnet, and Mamont banking Trojans.

title: Arkanix Stealer: a C++ & Python infostealer
summary: Kaspersky researchers analyze a C++ and Python stealer dubbed "Arkanix Stealer", which was active for several months, targeted wide range of data, was distributed as MaaS and offered referral program to its partners.

title: Divide and conquer: how the new Keenadu backdoor exposed links between major Android botnets
summary: Kaspersky experts have uncovered Keenadu, a sophisticated new backdoor targeting tablet firmware as well as system-level and Google Play apps. They also revealed connections between the world's most prolific Android botnets.

title: The game is over: when “free” comes at too high a price. What we know about RenEngine
summary: We disclose new details about campaigns involving RenEngine and HijackLoader malware. Since March 2025, attackers have been distributing the Lumma stealer in a complex chain of infections, and in February 2026, ongoing attacks using ACR Stealer became known.

title: Spam and phishing in 2025
summary: The report contains statistics on spam and phishing in 2025, outlining the main trends: phishing and scam QR codes, ClickFix attacks, ChatGPT subscription lures and others.

title: Stan Ghouls targeting Russia and Uzbekistan with NetSupport RAT
summary: We analyze the recent Stan Ghouls campaign targeting organizations in Russia and Uzbekistan: Java-based loaders, the NetSupport RAT, and a potential interest in IoT.

title: The Notepad++ supply chain attack — unnoticed execution chains and new IoCs
summary: Kaspersky GReAT experts discovered previously undocumented infection chains used in the Notepad++ supply chain attacks. The article provides new IoCs related to those incidents which employ DLL sideloading and Cobalt Strike Beacon delivery.

title: Supply chain attack on eScan antivirus: detecting and remediating malicious updates
summary: On January 20, Kaspersky solutions detected malware used in eScan antivirus supply chain attack. In this article we provide available information on the threat: indicators of compromise, threat hunting and mitigating tips, etc.

title: HoneyMyte updates CoolClient and deploys multiple stealers in recent campaigns
summary: Kaspersky researchers analyze updated CoolClient backdoor and new tools and scripts used in HoneyMyte (aka Mustang Panda or Bronze President) APT campaigns, including three variants of a browser data stealer.

title: The HoneyMyte APT evolves with a kernel-mode rootkit and a ToneShell backdoor
summary: Kaspersky discloses a 2025 HoneyMyte (aka Mustang Panda or Bronze President) APT campaign, which uses a kernel-mode rootkit to deliver and protect a ToneShell backdoor.
